mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand, which directly lifts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month RSAFS acceleration are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, restrictive monetary policy heightens the probability of a growth slowdown, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in payroll growth (PAYEMS) alongside a simultaneous decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a tight labor market with accelerating wage income, amplifying consumption and equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher when both series move in the predicted direction jointly compared to their independent marginal moves (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is accelerating, successive increases in the federal funds rate transmit into tighter credit conditions and lower equity valuations; the combined pressure of rising policy rates and rising consumer prices produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods of rising inflation, S&P 500 20-day forward returns following a federal funds rate increase are positive in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) signals stronger manufacturing momentum and capacity utilization, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO acceleration are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window signals labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades and equity drawdowns, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) of more than 5% signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking construction activity, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following a greater than 5% HOUST decline are positive in more than 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held above 5% for more than three consecutive months, the cumulative drag on credit-sensitive sectors outweighs earnings growth, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns during such periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) relative to baseline.
